home.title: Furano Outdoor Activities & River Tours | HOO Hokkaido

access.title: Access & Business Information

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: Questions about our activities, dates, group bookings or projects? Send us a message any time. We reply during business hours, 9:00-17:00 (JST).

column.title: Column | Outdoor Stories from Furano & Hokkaido

languagesSection.body: So that visitors coming to Hokkaido from overseas can plan an activity with confidence, Hokkaido Outdoor Organization publishes information in Japanese, English, Traditional Chinese, Simplified Chinese and Korean.
languagesSection.note: Our website and booking form are available in multiple languages. For the languages we can support on site and by phone, please ask us before you book.

Not just looking at Hokkaido.

Touch the moving water, listen to the forest, and feel the land of Hokkaido with your whole body.
Enjoy the nature of Furano, Biei and Asahikawa alongside experienced guides.

activityDetail.furanoNote: This is the program many people choose when they're searching for rafting or river activities in Furano. furano-relax-downriver.name: Furano Relax Downriver
furano-relax-downriver.catchcopy: See Hokkaido from the water, at the pace of the river.
furano-relax-downriver.summary: Our flagship river activity: a boat trip down the rivers of Furano, taking in the scenery from water level alongside an experienced guide.
furano-relax-downriver.imageAlt: A Relax Downriver trip on a river in Furano

meta.title: Furano Relax Downriver | River Activity in Furano
meta.description: Float down the rivers of Furano by boat with an experienced guide. From 2 people. A relaxed way to enjoy Hokkaido's nature on the water.

description: The Furano Relax Downriver is a river activity that takes you down the rivers of Furano by boat, close enough to the nature of Hokkaido to feel it.
The sky seen from water level, the forest along the banks, the sound of the current. From the river, Furano looks like nowhere you'll reach on foot.
An experienced guide reads the river on the day and leads the trip, so if it's your first time on the water, just talk to us.

features.title: Furano, seen from the river
features.body: The sky, the forest and the water's edge from an angle you'll never get from land. Drifting with the current is the point of the trip, not a means to an end.

features.title: A guide you can ask anything
features.body: Our representative has over 20 years as an outdoor guide, and our guides know these rivers. Ask us whatever you're unsure about, before you book or on the day.

features.title: An unhurried downriver trip
features.body: This isn't about speed or thrills. It's a program built around taking your time with the current and the nature of Furano.

recommendedFor: Travellers in Furano looking for a real encounter with nature
Families, friends and couples who want a memory to take home
Anyone curious about rafting or river trips, but not the wild kind
People who want to experience the Furano that photos can't capture

flow.body: Tell us your preferred date and group size, by form or phone.
We'll share the meeting point, what to bring and how the day runs.
Come to the meeting point we've given you.
Your guide explains the equipment and how the trip will go.
Head down the rivers of Furano with your guide.
The trip ends and you're free to carry on with your day.

notes: Trips may be cancelled or changed depending on the weather and river conditions.
If you have any concerns about your health, please talk to us in advance.
Sending the form does not confirm your booking. Your place is confirmed once we contact you.

faq.question: Can I join if it's my first time?
faq.answer: Yes. Your guide will explain the equipment and how the day works as you go. If anything worries you, just ask when you book.

faq.question: What is the minimum group size?
faq.answer: Bookings start from 2 people. For other group sizes, please get in touch.

faq.question: Can children take part?
faq.answer: The age range depends on the river conditions on the day. If you're coming with children, contact us in advance and let us know their ages.

faq.question: What should I wear and bring?
faq.answer: We'll tell you exactly what to wear and bring when you book.

biei-relax-downriver.name: Biei Relax Downriver
biei-relax-downriver.catchcopy: Biei, the town of rolling hills, seen from the water.
biei-relax-downriver.summary: A relaxed boat trip down a river in the Biei area, and a different side of a landscape most people only see from the hills.
biei-relax-downriver.imageAlt: A Relax Downriver trip on a river in Biei

meta.title: Biei Relax Downriver | River Activity in Biei
meta.description: A relaxed boat trip down a river in the Biei area. See the famous hill country of Biei from the water. From 2 people.

description: The Biei Relax Downriver is a guided boat trip down a river in the Biei area, and a chance to spend time in the landscape rather than beside it.
Biei is known for its hills and patchwork fields, but the view from the river is another thing entirely: water and forest close by, and time that moves at Hokkaido's pace.
It slots easily into a Biei and Furano itinerary. Tell us your plans and we'll work around them.

features.title: Biei's nature from the river
features.body: A quiet way to experience one of Hokkaido's most photographed areas, from an angle few visitors see.

features.title: Led by an experienced guide
features.body: Your guide reads the river as you go and leads the trip with safety in mind.

features.title: Easy to fit into your trip
features.body: It plans well alongside sightseeing in the Biei, Furano and Asahikawa areas.

recommendedFor: Travellers who want nature time alongside sightseeing in Biei
Anyone looking for more than the usual tourist route
People who want to enjoy nature quietly

flow.body: Tell us your preferred date and group size, by form or phone.

meta.description: A relaxed boat trip down a river in the Asahikawa area. Enjoy Hokkaido's nature just outside the city. From 2 people.

description: The Asahikawa Relax Downriver is a guided boat trip down a river in the Asahikawa area.
Hokkaido's second city is closer to rivers and forest than most people expect. Add a few hours on the water to your time in town.
It fits neatly into a trip that takes in Asahikawa, Biei and Furano.

features.title: The waterways of the Asahikawa area
features.body: A chance to get onto the river in a region better known for Asahiyama Zoo and city sightseeing.

features.title: Led by an experienced guide

features.title: Easy to reach from your base

organize-program.catchcopy: A nature experience built around your group.

description: The Organize Program is a made-to-order outdoor program, designed around what your group actually needs.
Corporate training, team building, a one-off event for friends, a community gathering: tell us the purpose and the number of people, and we'll put a plan together.
Enquiries at the "could you do something like this?" stage are welcome. Just get in touch.

features.title: Built around your goals
features.body: We shape the program around your group size, purpose and available time.

features.title: An experienced guide from the planning stage

recommendedFor: Companies planning training or team building

flow.body: Tell us your purpose, group size and preferred dates.
We listen, then come back with a program proposal and a quote.
Once the details are settled, we confirm your booking.
Your guide runs the program as planned.

notes: Programs may be cancelled or changed depending on the weather and river conditions.
The price depends on the content and group size. Please contact us to discuss.

faq.question: How many people can you accommodate?
faq.answer: It depends on the group size and the program. Contact us with the number of people you have in mind.

faq.question: How flexible is the content?
faq.answer: We'll work with your purpose and the time you have. It's fine if nothing is decided yet.

furanoActivity.title:
Looking for things to do
and nature to explore in Furano
furanoActivity.lead: Furano is more than lavender fields and beautiful hills. From the water, you can feel this landscape rather than just look at it. HOO is based here, and here's how we'd help you choose.
furanoActivity.introTitle: Nature experiences you can have in Furano
introParagraphs: Furano is best known for lavender fields and ski slopes. But there is more to it than that. A short drive from town, rivers and forest open into the kind of scenery Hokkaido is made of.

A downriver trip, drifting along in a boat, brings you to a Furano that sightseeing on foot never reaches. The sound of the water, the smell of the forest, the breeze across the surface, and the view changing as you move: none of it exists anywhere but on the river.
HOO is based in Furano and runs river activities here as well as in the Biei and Asahikawa areas.
furanoActivity.styleTitle: Choosing a Furano activity by who you're with

styleCards.title: With family
styleCards.body: Because the Relax Downriver is about taking your time rather than chasing speed, families ask about it often. Whether children can join depends on their age, so please talk to us first.

styleCards.title: With friends or a partner
styleCards.body: The view of Furano from the water is the kind of memory a photo can't quite hold. Bookings start from 2 people, so it works well for a trip for two.

styleCards.title: With a group
styleCards.body: Our Organize Program is built around your goals, whether that's training or an event. Tell us the numbers and the purpose, and we'll propose something.

furanoActivity.whyTitle: Why a river activity, and why now
whyParagraphs: If the view from a car window or an observation deck is nature you look at, a river activity is nature you go into. Drifting with the current alongside a guide, Furano works its way into your memory through all five senses.

HOO's downriver trips aren't about intensity. They're built around taking the river and the scenery slowly, and plenty of people who wouldn't call themselves outdoorsy choose them as their first time on the water.
checkPoints: Whether a trip runs depends on the weather and river conditions, so make sure we can reach you as your travel dates approach
What to wear and bring changes with the season. Follow the details we send when you book
Meeting points vary by activity. Check yours, and how you'll get there, in advance
If you have any concerns about your health, talk to us before you book
furanoActivity.recommendedLead: Our flagship program is the Furano Relax Downriver, and we run trips in the Biei and Asahikawa areas too.
furanoActivity.guideTitle: Our guide, and how we think about safety
furanoActivity.guideBody: Shota Kose, HOO's representative, has more than 20 years as an outdoor guide. He is a Hokkaido Governor-certified Outdoor Guide (Rafting / Canoe) and holds Rescue3 SRT-1 and Advanced First Aid certification, with guiding experience on rivers across Japan. Whether a trip runs is decided on the day, based on the weather and the state of the river, and we would rather not run than push it.

faq.question: Do I need to book in advance?
faq.answer: Yes, all HOO activities require advance booking. Once you know your date, contact us by form or phone as early as you can.

faq.question: How do I fit this into my Furano itinerary?
faq.answer: We share the duration and meeting point when you book, so if your plans are already set, send them along with your enquiry and we'll suggest a time that works.

faq.question: Can I take part outside summer?
faq.answer: The season depends on the state of the river. Once you know when you'd like to come, check with us by form or phone.

faq.question: Are any activities suitable for children?
faq.answer: The age range depends on the activity and the river conditions on the day. Contact us in advance with your children's ages and we'll let you know.

furanoActivity.ctaTitle:
See Furano from the river.
We'll take you there.

furanoRafting.title:
Looking for rafting or
a river experience in Furano
furanoRafting.lead: If you searched for "Furano rafting" and landed here, this page is for you. Here's an honest look at the choices, and at what HOO actually runs on the rivers of Furano.
furanoRafting.introTitle: What to know before you pick a river trip in Furano
introParagraphs: "Rafting" is used loosely for almost any trip that takes a boat down a river, but the experiences behind the word are very different. Some run white water for the thrill of it. Others move gently, and are about the scenery and the life around the water.

What HOO runs in Furano is closer to the second kind: a program we call the Relax Downriver. It isn't a white-water run. You ride the current and take in the nature of Furano at an easy pace, which makes it a comfortable first time on a river.
As you read on, it helps to keep asking yourself what kind of day you actually want.
furanoRafting.compareTitle: What to look at when comparing river trips

comparePoints.title: Check what kind of trip it is
comparePoints.body: River trips range from ones built around the rapids to ones built around the view. Deciding what kind of time you want first makes everything else easier.

comparePoints.title: Check the area and the meeting point
comparePoints.body: Even within "the Furano area", operators use different rivers and different meeting points. Check yours against your itinerary and how you'll travel.

comparePoints.title: Check who can take part
comparePoints.body: Age limits and group size requirements vary. If you're bringing children or a large group, ask before you book.

comparePoints.title: Check the guide's experience and qualifications
comparePoints.body: When nature sets the terms, a guide's experience, qualifications and attitude to safety are worth knowing about.

furanoRafting.programLead: A river activity that takes you gently down the rivers of Furano, with a guide alongside you.
furanoRafting.beforeTitle: What to sort out before your first trip

beforeCards.title: What to wear and bring
beforeCards.body: You may well get wet, so what you wear and bring should suit the season. We send full details when you book, so follow those and you'll be set.

beforeCards.title: Weather and river conditions
beforeCards.body: Whether a river trip runs depends on the weather and the state of the river. Your guide decides based on the conditions, and we'll let you know in advance if we have to cancel or change anything.

beforeCards.title: Ask us what's on your mind
beforeCards.body: Not a strong swimmer, not sure about your fitness, wondering whether to bring the kids? Ask before you book, and we'll give you a straight answer.

furanoRafting.guideTitle: Guided by someone who has worked rivers across Japan
guideParagraphs: Shota Kose, HOO's representative, has more than 20 years as a river guide, on the Yoshino River in Shikoku, the Tone River in Gunma, the Mu River in Hokkaido and elsewhere.
He is a Hokkaido Governor-certified Outdoor Guide (Rafting / Canoe) and holds Rescue3 SRT-1 and Advanced First Aid certification, reading the river as it is on the day and running trips accordingly.
furanoRafting.guideImageAlt: A HOO guide leading a trip on a river in Hokkaido
furanoRafting.guideLink: Read about our approach to safety
furanoRafting.faqTitle: Questions about river trips in Furano

faq.question: How is the Relax Downriver different from rafting?
faq.answer: "Rafting" tends to be used as a catch-all for going down a river in a boat. HOO's Relax Downriver isn't about chasing thrills: it's a downriver trip built around taking the current and the scenery slowly. If you're not sure it matches what you have in mind, just ask us.

faq.question: Can I join if I can't swim?
faq.answer: Talk to us in advance about anything that concerns you. On the day, your guide will explain the equipment and how to spend your time on the water before you set off.

faq.question: Should I just wear something I don't mind getting wet?
faq.answer: What to wear and bring depends on the season and the conditions on the day, so we send full details when you book. If you'd like to know earlier, get in touch.

faq.question: What happens if the weather looks bad?
faq.answer: Your guide decides whether to run the trip, and in what form, based on the weather and river conditions. If we have to cancel or change anything, we'll contact you in advance.

relatedLinks: How to choose an outdoor experience in Furano

Your first downriver trip,

about.title: About HOO
about.lead: Hokkaido Outdoor Organization (HOO) is an outdoor organisation based in Furano, offering nature experiences across Hokkaido.
about.missionTitle:
Connecting people with Hokkaido's nature,
and with moments that move them.
about.missionBody:
We create experiences that connect you with nature,
not just another day of sightseeing.
visionParagraphs: What we set out to do is bring people and the nature of Hokkaido together.
Not just to look at the scenery, but to feel the breath of the land, touch the moving water, and find yourself somewhere in the middle of it.
We want to be the people who make that meeting happen: the bridge between nature and the feeling it leaves behind.
about.riversBody: Years of river guiding on the Yoshino River in Shikoku, the Tone River in Gunma, the Mu River in Hokkaido and other rivers across Japan.
about.projectsLink: See HOO's projects: river management and local development

guide.careerYears: Over 20 years as an outdoor guide
guide.imageAlt: Shota Kose, HOO's representative, guiding an outdoor experience in Furano
guide.safetyImageAlt: A HOO guide leading a river trip with safety in mind
profile: More than 20 years as an outdoor guide, with river guiding experience on the Yoshino River in Shikoku, the Tone River in Gunma, the Mu River in Hokkaido and other rivers across Japan.
Now based back home in Hokkaido, building outdoor experiences that make the most of what this place already is.
qualifications: Advanced First Aid / Advanced Life Saving certification

reasons.title: Over 20 years as an outdoor guide
reasons.body: Your guide has worked rivers across Japan, from the Yoshino River in Shikoku to the Tone River in Gunma and the Mu River in Hokkaido.

reasons.title: Hokkaido Governor-certified Outdoor Guide
reasons.body: Certified for rafting and canoe, and building programs on a deep familiarity with Hokkaido's rivers.

reasons.title: Rescue3 SRT-1 and Advanced First Aid
reasons.body: Specialist qualifications in swiftwater rescue and life saving underpin how we run every trip.

reasons.title: Rooted in Hokkaido's nature and communities
reasons.body: We also work on river management, conservation and local development, alongside the communities we belong to.

reasons.title: Small groups welcome
reasons.body: Bookings start from 2 people. If you're unsure about numbers or content, just ask.

projects.title: HOO's projects
projects.lead:
Running activities alone won't protect the future of these rivers or the communities around them.
HOO works on three fronts to look after Hokkaido's waterways and the places they run through.
projects.ctaTitle:
The future of the rivers,
and of the places around them.
projects.ctaLead: We welcome enquiries about our projects and about working together.

river-management.title: River management and conservation
river-management.summary: Keeping Hokkaido's rivers places people can enjoy safely, and passing them on to the next generation.
river-management.imageAlt: River management and conservation work in Hokkaido
description: Rivers hold up both everyday life in the community and the outdoor experiences we offer. HOO doesn't just use the water: we look after it, taking on the management and conservation of the riverside.

items.title: A point of contact for waterfront use
items.body: We field enquiries about using the waterfront and act as a bridge between the community, users and local government.

items.title: Clear guidelines for use
items.body: We work on plain, usable guidelines so that everyone can enjoy the water with confidence.

items.title: Maintenance and operation of facilities
items.body: We maintain and operate riverside equipment and facilities, keeping the environment safe and comfortable.

community-space.title: Places to relax and meet
community-space.summary: Turning the riverside into somewhere locals and visitors can unwind and meet, through events and partnerships.
community-space.imageAlt: Creating places to relax and meet by the water
description: The waterfront isn't only for activities. Creating places where local people and visitors naturally gather and settle in is a real part of HOO's work.

items.title: Planning and coordinating local events
items.body: We plan and coordinate community events on the water's edge, giving people a reason to come together.

items.title: Listening to local voices
items.body: We take the time to hear what local residents want, and let that shape what we build.

items.title: Working with nearby attractions
items.body: We partner with nearby attractions and businesses to open up new ways to enjoy the waterfront.

regional-tourism.title: Tourism and the local economy
regional-tourism.summary: Using nature experiences as a way in, and building tourism and economic energy in Furano and beyond.
regional-tourism.imageAlt: Supporting tourism and the local economy in Furano
description: Outdoor experiences bring people to a region and put money into it. HOO's work doesn't stop at running trips: we work to strengthen local tourism and the economy around it.

items.title: Promoting and selling local products
items.body: By promoting and selling products that carry the character of the region, we help keep value circulating locally.

items.title: Developing people who will carry the region forward
items.body: We invest in the people who will shape the region's future, so that growth here can last.

safety.title: Our approach to safety
safety.lead:
Nothing is ever guaranteed when nature sets the terms.
So we read the conditions, and we don't push our luck.
safety.note: For anything not covered here, such as equipment details or the conditions for running a trip, we'll explain when you book, or you can ask us directly.
safety.guideTitle:
A guide who knows the river,
reading it as it is today.
safety.guideBody: The same river changes from one day to the next with the water level and the weather. Shota Kose, our representative, has over 20 years of experience as an outdoor guide and runs each program based on the conditions he finds that day.
safety.flowTitle: From booking to the day itself
safety.flowLead: Meeting points, what to bring, how long it takes: we cover all of it when you book.
safety.ctaTitle:
If something's on your mind,
ask before you book.
safety.ctaLead: Health, age, not being a confident swimmer: whatever it is, talk to us in advance.

commitments.title: A briefing before you start
commitments.body: Your guide runs through how the day will go and what to watch for, so first-timers can clear up any questions before setting off.

commitments.title: Equipment and how the day runs
commitments.body: We explain the equipment you'll need and how the day works when you book and again on the day. Ask us anything in advance.

commitments.title: An experienced guide with you
commitments.body: A guide with real river experience goes with you, reading the conditions as the trip unfolds.

commitments.title: Decisions based on weather and river conditions
commitments.body: Your guide decides whether to run the trip, and in what form, based on the weather and the state of the river. We never force a trip through.

commitments.title: What we ask of you
commitments.body: So that everyone can enjoy the day safely, we ask you to follow your guide's instructions on the water.

commitments.title: Health concerns: talk to us first
commitments.body: If you have a medical condition or any concern about your health, get in touch before you book and we'll advise you.

flowSteps.body: Tell us the activity, dates and group size you have in mind, by form or phone.

flowSteps.step: Meeting point and what to bring
flowSteps.body: We'll send you the meeting point, what to wear and what to bring for your booking.
Come to the meeting point we've given you and check in.
Your guide explains the equipment, how the day runs and what to watch for.
Enjoy Hokkaido's rivers and its nature alongside your guide.
The activity ends and you're free to carry on with your day.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.lead: The questions we're asked most often before a trip. If yours isn't here, just get in touch.
faq.helpBody:
You can reach us by phone during business hours, 9:00-17:00 (Japan Standard Time).
Outside those hours, the contact form is open around the clock.

items.question: Can I join if it's my first time?
items.answer: Yes, first-timers are welcome. On the day, your guide will explain the equipment and how everything works as you go. If anything is worrying you, just ask before you book.

items.question: What is the minimum group size?
items.answer: Every activity starts from 2 people. If you'd like to join alone or bring a large group, please talk to us in advance.

items.question: What should I wear and bring?
items.answer: What to wear and bring changes with the season and the state of the river, so we send full details when you book. Ask us in advance if you'd like to know sooner.

items.question: What happens if it rains?
items.answer: Your guide decides whether to run the trip, and in what form, based on the weather and river conditions. If we have to cancel or change anything, we'll contact you in advance, so get in touch even if the forecast looks doubtful.

items.question: Can children take part?
items.answer: The age range depends on the activity and the river conditions on the day. If you'd like to bring children, contact us in advance and let us know their ages.

items.question: Where do we meet?
items.answer: We give you the meeting point in full when you book. It differs by area (Furano, Biei or Asahikawa), so check the details for your booking.

items.question: How far in advance should I book?
items.answer: All activities require advance booking. Once your date is set, contact us as early as you can. For last-minute bookings, call us and we'll see what's possible.

items.question: What is your cancellation policy?
items.answer: The conditions and any cancellation fees are explained when you book. If your plans change afterwards, let us know as soon as you can.

items.question: Do you take group bookings?
items.answer: Yes. For groups we offer the Organize Program, built around your purpose. Tell us the number of people and what you have in mind, and we'll propose something.

items.question: Do you run trips outside Furano?
items.answer: Yes. As well as Furano, we run Relax Downriver trips in the Biei and Asahikawa areas. Choose whichever fits your itinerary.

access.title: Access & business information
access.lead: Where HOO is based, and how to reach us.
access.meetingBody: The meeting point for each activity depends on the area (Furano, Biei or Asahikawa) and on the river conditions that day. It may not be the address above, so please check the details we send when you book. If anything is unclear, call us or use the contact form.
access.mapNote: Meeting points differ by area. We'll give you full details when you book.

reservation.title: Booking form
reservation.lead:
Tell us which activity you'd like and when.
We'll review your request and confirm your booking by getting in touch.
reservation.phoneNote: Phone lines open 9:00-17:00 (Japan Standard Time) / Outside these hours, please use the form
reservation.notice: Sending this form does not confirm your booking. We'll review your request, and your place is confirmed once we contact you.
reservation.successBody:
We've received your booking request.
We'll review it and get back to you shortly.
reservation.successStrong: Your booking is confirmed once we contact you.
reservation.successNote: If you haven't heard from us after a while, please give us a call.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.lead:
Questions about an activity, a group booking, or one of our projects?
Get in touch any time. The form is open around the clock.
contact.successBody:
We'll review it and get back to you.
Thank you for your patience.

column.title: Column
column.lead: Useful things to know about the outdoors in Furano, Biei and Asahikawa, from the guides who work here.
column.empty: Articles are on the way. Please check back soon.
column.notAvailable: This article is not currently available in this language.
column.defaultSupervisor: Shota Kose (HOO representative, over 20 years as an outdoor guide)

sections.title: 1. Our approach
body: Hokkaido Outdoor Organization ("we", "us") recognises the importance of the personal information entrusted to us by visitors to this website. We comply with the laws and regulations governing the protection of personal information, and we take care in how we collect, use and manage it.

sections.title: 2. Information we collect
body: When you submit our booking form or contact form, we collect your name, your name in katakana or Roman letters, date of birth, country or region, phone number, email address, preferred language for correspondence, preferred date, number of participants, the area where you are staying, and any other information you enter into the form.

sections.title: 3. How we use it
body: We use the personal information we collect for the following purposes.
- To confirm, arrange and finalise your booking
- To send you important information about your participation, such as whether an activity will run
- As reference material for improving the quality of our service

sections.title: 4. Disclosure to third parties
body: We do not provide personal information to third parties without your consent, except where required by law.

sections.title: 5. Managing personal information
body: We manage the personal information we collect appropriately and work to prevent leakage, loss or damage. Once information has served its purpose, we dispose of it appropriately.

sections.title: 6. Use of external services
body: This website may use external services to process form submissions and to understand how the site is used. Information handled by those services is subject to their own privacy policies.

sections.title: 7. Disclosure, correction and deletion
body: If you ask us to disclose, correct or delete your personal information, we will verify your identity and respond promptly.

sections.title: 8. Contact
body: For questions about how we handle personal information, please use the contact details at the bottom of this page.

sections.title: 9. Changes to this policy
body: This policy may be revised without notice in response to changes in the law or in how we operate. Any revision takes effect once it is published on this page.

terms.title: Booking information & guidelines
terms.lead: A few things to know so you can enjoy your activity with confidence. Please read before you book.

sections.title: Booking
items: Every activity starts from 2 people. For other group sizes, please talk to us.
Sending the form does not confirm your booking. We'll review your request, and your place is confirmed once we contact you.
The meeting point, what to bring and how the day runs are all explained when you book.

sections.title: Deciding whether a trip runs
items: Depending on the weather and river conditions, your guide may cancel a trip or change what it involves.
If we have to cancel or change anything, we'll contact you in advance.

sections.title: Cancellations
items: Cancellation conditions and any cancellation fees are explained when you book.
If your plans change after booking, please let us know as soon as you can.

sections.title: On the day
items: For everyone's safety, please follow your guide's instructions throughout.
If you are feeling unwell, or have a medical condition or any concern about your health, talk to us before you book.
We may decline participation if you have been drinking.
Age ranges and participation requirements vary by activity and by the conditions on the day. Please contact us in advance.

terms.contactBody: For anything not covered here, please ask us through the contact form or by phone.

links.about: About HOO (mission, vision and our guide)

notFound.heading: We couldn't find that page
notFound.body:
The page you're looking for may have moved or been removed.
Try starting again from the home page or the activities list.
